summary: Focused UI verification of the new Admin Sales Dashboard tab. All acceptance criteria pass: Dashboard is the FIRST and DEFAULT-selected admin tab, 4 stat cards show correct values ($79.99 / 1 / $79.99 / $79.99), revenue chart renders with 30 day-columns and a visible bar for today, tooltip on hover shows $ and order count, Best Sellers lists Windows 11 Pro #1 with '1 sold' and $79.99. Regression on the other 5 admin tabs (products/orders/leads/keys/emails) all load content correctly; navigating back to Dashboard works. No React errors/warnings related to the dashboard component.
critical_code_review_comments: DashboardTab.jsx line 42: api.get('/api/admin/dashboard') has no .catch() — if the endpoint fails the component is stuck on TabSpinner forever. Add an error state (similar to other tabs) so admins see a retry/error message.

Two non-blocking recharts console warnings emitted on first paint: 'width(-1) and height(-1) of chart should be greater than 0'. Caused by ResponsiveContainer measuring its parent before flex layout settles. Optional fix: give the wrapping div a minWidth/minHeight or set an aspect ratio on ResponsiveContainer. Chart still renders correctly so this is cosmetic.
Two console 401s observed on the /login page from background /api/auth/me probe before authentication. Pre-existing and unrelated to the dashboard feature.
seed_data_creation: None — used existing seeded order ($79.99 Windows 11 Pro).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Sales Dashboard tab feature is fully verified visually and functionally. Screenshot in .screenshots/dashboard.png shows the full dashboard with stat cards, bar chart, tooltip and best-sellers all rendering correctly. The only minor polish item is adding an error/catch handler to api.get('/api/admin/dashboard') in DashboardTab.jsx — currently it silently hangs on TabSpinner if the request fails.
